keyPoints: Definition: The Cartesian product of two sets A and B, denoted by A × B, is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a ∈ A and b ∈ B.
Properties: A × ∅ = ∅, A × A ≠ ∅, and (A × B) × C = A × (B × C) for any sets A, B, and C.

Example: Let A = {1, 2} and B = {a, b}. Then A × B = {(1, a), (1, b), (2, a), (2, b)}. The relation R ⊆ A × B is defined by R = {(a, b) | a is the name of a student and b is the grade of that student},
